title: High Performance Computing
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
High Performance Computing
The specialized High Performance Computing (HPC) team can help design network integration and system infrastructure in academic labs. They can also support research labs.
NYU Shanghai HPC Committee Members:
Allann Bellenger, Head of IT Services Management, IT department
Anqi Qian, Vice Provost for Research, Director of Research Institutes
Guangchao Hu, HPC Specialist, IT department
Hanghui Chen, Associate Professor of Physics, NYU Shanghai; Global Network Associate Professor, NYU
Pan Chang, Senior Director of Information Technology, IT department
Shengbin Lu, Assistant Director of Network, IT department
William Glover, Co-Director of the NYU-ECNU Center for Computational Chemistry; Associate Professor of Chemistry, NYU Shanghai; Global Network Associate Professor, NYU
Xiang Sun, Assistant Professor of Chemistry, NYU Shanghai; Global Network Assistant Professor, NYU
Xiaojing Zu, Assistant Provost and Library Director
Yik-Cheung Tam, Associate Director of the Center for Data Science, Professor of Practice in Computer Science
* Alphabetically ordered by lname
NYU faculty and staff, and faculty-sponsored students.
This policy governs access to the NYU Shanghai HPC cluster and applies to all community members using or requesting HPC resources, including faculty, researchers, and (PhD) students. It covers account eligibility and creation, usage rules, (PhD) student transition protections, resource limits, and mandatory security and compliance requirements. For the full policy, access procedures, and compliance obligations, refer to the NYU Shanghai IT HPC Policy page.
Please fill out the Request Form.
Submit a request on support.nyu.edu
Send a request through WeChat
Email shanghai.it.help@nyu.edu
Visit the IT Service Center: Room N525 @ New Bund Campus
Check on the NYUSH HPC Docs for more information.
